action.reasoning: Observation: Previous training produced NaN loss from batch 0, leading to model learning nothing and macro_f1=0.0. val_macro_f1 was 0.002, worse than random. Analysis: NaN loss could be caused by class weights, mixed precision, or DataParallel issues. Decision: Simplify training: remove class weights, disable mixed precision, add anomaly detection and NaN checks, use gradient clipping, and start with a very small learning rate (1e-5). Train on 10% data for 3 epochs to get loss finite and a meaningful macro_f1 score. Then submit eval to measure improvement and iteration time.
